stem: A pupil has finished writing a program that calculates a final mark from a percentage between 0 and 100.
model_answer: Iterative testing happens during development: after each small change, run the part of the program that just changed to check it still works. Final testing happens once the program is complete: run a planned set of tests covering normal data (e.g. 73), boundary data (0, 100) and erroneous data (-5, 150, 'cat'). For each test, expected output is recorded and compared against actual. Bugs found in iterative testing are usually cheap to fix; bugs found only at final testing are more expensive because more code may need to change.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award marks for naming iterative + final, the three test-data classes (normal/boundary/erroneous), and an applied example.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe the difference between iterative and final testing, and give one example of normal, boundary and erroneous test data the pupil could use.

mark_points.text: iterative testing happens during development; final testing once the program is complete
normal data example (e.g. 73) and boundary data example (0 OR 100)
